rationale: The cited paper is discussed in a dedicated theoretical section, not just listed in background citations. The citing paper explicitly compares its mechanism to Park et al.'s option-framing account and uses its own Study 4 results to argue that a perceived positive price-quality relationship is a necessary condition for that effect. It then proposes boundary conditions for when the Park et al. effect should or should not generalize, which is a meaningful conceptual qualification.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Park et al. (2000) as a key prior mechanism and then qualifies its scope by arguing that the option-framing effect depends on a positive perceived price-quality link. In this way, it sharpens the conditions under which the earlier effect should hold.
